mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, which increases household income and consumer confidence, driving equity valuations higher through elevated earnings expectations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138102.0 thousand as of July 2013
failure_condition: Payroll growth remains above 150k persons monthly yet S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 3.5% signals monetary tightening that raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS remains above 3.5% for 3+ consecutive months yet S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales and housing starts signals robust consumer and construction demand, which increases corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both proxies to move in the same direction simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 382906.0 million and HOUST at 1006.0 thousand as of July 2013
failure_condition: Both RSAFS and HOUST show month-over-month growth simultaneously in 3+ consecutive months yet S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index decline of more than 0.5% month-over-month signals supply-side contraction and reduced corporate output capacity, which depresses earnings growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines > 0.5% month-over-month yet S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 5.2% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power, which depresses equity valuations through lower expected corporate revenues over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market weakness or recession onset
failure_condition: UNRATE remains above 5.2% for 3+ consecutive months yet S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI rising above 2.5% year-over-year combined with federal funds rate below 2.0% signals real monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations through positive real discount rate compression over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 211.68 and FEDFUNDS at 3.94% as of July 2013
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with moderate inflation
failure_condition: Real rates remain negative (CPIAUCSL growth > FEDFUNDS) for 3+ consecutive months yet S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 decelerating to below 2.0% annualized signals late-cycle slowdown and reduced corporate earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11677.4 billion chained 2017 dollars as of April 2013
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth remains below 2.0% annualized for 2+ consecutive quarters yet S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST rising above 1000 thousand units monthly combined with retail sales growth above 0.3% month-over-month signals robust consumer and construction demand that drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate revenue expectations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1006.0 thousand and RSAFS at 382906.0 million as of July 2013
regime_hint: housing recovery and consumer spending acceleration
failure_condition: Both HOUST > 1000 and RSAFS growth > 0.3% month-over-month occur simultaneously for 3+ consecutive months yet S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.
